element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn pitch, yaw, and roll in zero-gravity environment
Basic robot combat; energy management introduced
Core level goal; introduces time pressure via self-destruct countdown
Weapon switching strategy; limited ammo management
Wayfinding tool to prevent disorientation in complex 3D spaces
Darkness management; essential for visibility in mines
Resource management; safe zones within danger
Optional wayfinding companion; reduces navigation friction
Introduces dynamic threat scaling; destruction of generators reduces spawning
Puzzle-combat hybrid; shooting panels opens doors, removes walls, deactivates force fields
Optional bonus objectives; adds secondary goals alongside reactor destruction
Replaces reactor in boss levels; significant combat escalation
Optional high-risk/reward content; no save points; risky but power-up rich
Alternative to energy-consuming weapons; sustained fire capability

typical_relief_triggers: Reactor destruction and subsequent escape phase
typical_tension_triggers: Encountering robot generators spawning continuous threats
Locked colored doors blocking direct path (forcing exploration)
Boss robot encounters at every 4th level
Control panel traps or force field ambushes
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical tension with clear escalation: exploration and discovery (moderate tension) → reactor location and approach (rising tension) → reactor destruction (peak tension with time pressure) → escape sequence (sustained tension with navigation complexity). Difficulty increases across six star systems; boss encounters provide intermediate climaxes.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden doors behind which teleporters lead to secret levels; colored key-doors gate optional paths; off-the-beaten-path rooms contain power-ups and hostages
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Teleporters hidden behind doors in regular levels warp to current star system's secret level; secret levels unlocked only when teleporters discovered; not required for completion

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to 6DOF movement and basic combat against simple robots; single-room encounters; straightforward reactor locations; emphasis on mastery of three-dimensional piloting
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Complex multi-room mines with colored key progression; introduction of robot generators and control panels; Guide-Bot and hostage objectives; first boss encounter (Level 4); increased robot variety and density
difficulty_curve.late_game: Six star systems with progressively themed and complex level design; boss encounters every 4th level; secret level access incentivizes thorough exploration; weapon scarcity and energy management become critical
notable_spikes: Level 4: First boss robot replaces reactor; significant combat jump
Level 8: Second star system boss; increased robot AI variety
Level 12: Third star system boss; mine complexity peaks
Levels 16, 20, 24, 28: Subsequent boss encounters with escalating difficulty
Secret levels: High-risk optional content with intense enemy density and limited save access

notable_level_patterns.description: Standard level structure (80% of game): Player must navigate mine, find reactor core, destroy it, and escape before self-destruct. Colored doors gate shortcuts; exploration reveals power-ups and hostages.
Every 4th level (6 total): Boss robot replaces reactor as primary objective. Requires sustained combat and dodging in 3D space; completion unlocks next star system.
Bonus off-grid content (20% of game): Accessed via hidden teleporters; no save points; high density of power-ups; optional but incentivizes thorough exploration of regular levels.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: One per star system (6 secret levels total)

notable_level_patterns.description: Rooms interconnected by doors requiring keys or shooting; automap and Guide-Bot essential for navigation; robot ambushes in corridors; control panels trigger environmental hazards or access routes.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Mid-to-late levels in all star systems
